senses.definition: used to describe a person who seems to be incapable of doing something due to a lack of necessary resources or skills
used to say that someone is extremely bad at doing something

examples.x: He can't paint for toffee!
She can't sing for toffee.
She can't dance for toffee, but she's always the first on the dance floor at parties.
I can't play chess for toffee, but I enjoy watching others play.

senses.definition: a person or thing that excels at doing only one thing

examples.x: He's a one-trick pony when it comes to cooking, but his spaghetti is legendary.
The company needs employees who can handle a variety of tasks; we don't want one-trick ponies.
Don't be a one-trick pony in your job search; develop a range of skills to stand out.

senses.definition: used to say that one did not have much experience with something and therefore is not very good at it

examples.x: I used to be a great piano player, but I'm out of practice now.
After taking a break from coding, I feel like I'm out of practice with programming.
She used to speak French fluently, but after not using it for years, she's out of practice.

senses.definition: having little knowledge or experience of a particular situation or activity

examples.x: He's a talented musician, but he's still wet behind the ears when it comes to performing on stage.
Don't expect her to handle complex negotiations; she's still wet behind the ears in the business world.
The recent college graduate may be smart, but he's yet wet behind the ears when it comes to practical work experience.

senses.definition: to no longer be able to do a certain activity as well as one did in the past

examples.x: His last album flopped; he seems to be losing his touch.
He used to be a fantastic chef, but he seems to have lost his touch in the kitchen lately.
The famous painter produced incredible works in his youth, but in his later years, he lost his touch.
Once a brilliant athlete, he has lost his touch on the field, and his performance has suffered.

word_list.pronunciation: nˌɑːt ˈiːvən ɔːɹ nˈoʊ ðə mˈiːnɪŋ ʌvðə wˈɜːd

senses.definition: to lack knowledge or understanding of a particular concept or term

examples.x: When it comes to modern technology, my grandparents don't know the meaning of the word.
I had to laugh when he didn’t know the meaning of the word, even though he claimed to be an expert.
She said she’d studied the topic, but she didn’t know the meaning of the word.
He didn’t even know the meaning of the word when I asked him to explain it.

senses.definition: an unscientific or inaccurate estimate or method

examples.x: He didn't have any data to back up his claim; it was just a finger in the air.
The CEO's decision seemed like a finger in the air rather than a well-thought-out strategy.
I don't trust his estimates; he's just putting his finger in the air and hoping for the best.
The forecast was nothing more than a finger in the air, with no real analysis behind it.
We need more than just a finger in the air to make important decisions; we need concrete evidence and analysis.

word_list.word: jack of all trades, master of none
word_list.pronunciation: dʒˈæk ʌv ˈɔːl tɹˈeɪdz mˈæstɚɹ ʌv nˈʌn

senses.definition: a versatile and adaptable individual who can handle a variety of tasks effectively
